metadata.title: 11th Pass Test Coverage Improvement — Regression Tests for Infrastructure Learnings
metadata.approach: TDD via /self-learning-iterative-coder — RED tests first, GREEN implementation, VERIFY

tier.name: staging
tier.reason: Config consistency, YAML parsing, data model — no model loading, no cloud

tier.name: prod
tier.reason: Slower tests involving subprocess mocks or file I/O

expert.domain: Test organization, coverage gaps, tier assignment

analysis.finding: The region migration from europe-west4 to us-central1 touched 15+ config files but only test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py was updated. No test verifies that ALL config files agree on the same region. A grep-based cross-file consistency test is missing. This is the #1 gap — every migration creates stale references that only surface at launch time.

The anomaly detector has 14 tests but no test for alert DEDUPLICATION. The _already_fired set prevents duplicate alerts, but no test verifies that calling check_all() twice for the same state returns zero alerts the second time. This is critical — duplicate alerts would overwhelm the notification system.

All monitoring tests (test_job_manifest.py, test_sky_queue_parser.py, test_anomaly_detector.py) are pure Python with zero external deps. They belong in staging tier. Verify they have no model_loading or slow markers.

Region consistency tests should be in tests/v2/unit/deployment/ alongside test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py. Cost guardrail tests should be in tests/v2/unit/finops/. Monitoring edge-case tests extend the existing tests/v2/unit/monitoring/ directory.

recommendations.rec: Create tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_region_consistency.py for ALL cross-file region checks
Extend test_anomaly_detector.py with deduplication and edge-case tests
Create tests/v2/unit/finops/test_cost_guardrails.py for budget/cost tests
Verify all new tests run in staging tier (no model_loading, no slow markers)

expert.domain: GCP resources, region consistency, YAML contracts

analysis.finding: After two region migrations (europe-north1 -> europe-west4 -> us-central1), the risk of stale region references is extremely high. The project has config files in: configs/cloud/, configs/cloud/regions/, configs/factorial/, deployment/skypilot/, deployment/pulumi/gcp/, scripts/, .env.example, and .sky.yaml. A single stale reference causes cross-region egress ($89/month) or FAILED_SETUP (wrong GAR URL). No comprehensive cross-file check exists.

The YAML contract was updated to allow A100 + A100-80GB on 2026-03-28. The old test_contract_gcp_does_not_include_a100 was likely updated, but we need NEW tests that verify the ordering invariant: L4 entries MUST precede A100 entries in all region configs and ordered: blocks. Without this, a misconfigured region config could default to A100 (5x cost).

scripts/preflight_gcp.py has GAR_IMAGE hardcoded as a string constant: "us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/minivess-mlops/minivess/base:latest". If the region changes again, this constant must be manually updated. A test should verify this constant matches gcp_spot.yaml's docker_image.

recommendations.rec: Grep-based test: zero stale europe-west4 references in active configs
Cross-file region consistency: all configs agree on the same region
GAR URL consistency: preflight_gcp.py matches gcp_spot.yaml
GPU ordering invariant: L4 before A100 before A100-80GB in all region configs
Pulumi region matches gcp_spot.yaml region

analysis.finding: The anomaly detector tests cover basic cases but miss the EXACT failure scenarios from passes 4, 10, and 11. We should have tests that reconstruct the actual observed states: - Pass 4: 8 identical FAILED_SETUP jobs (kill switch should trigger) - Pass 10: Job running 12h for a 5-min task (duration cancel) - Pass 11: 3 jobs PENDING for 10h (pending timeout) Each test should verify the EXACT alert type, severity, and action.

Missing edge cases in sky_queue_parser: - RECOVERING status (spot preemption recovery in progress) - CANCELLING status (user or auto-cancel in progress) - Multiword REQUESTED field ("1x[A100-80GB:1, L4:1][Spot]") - Duration parsing for "1d 4h 55m 45s" (day-scale durations) The parser handles these in code but tests only cover basic cases.

No test verifies the full manifest lifecycle: 1. Create manifest with PENDING jobs 2. Update statuses from queue output 3. Run anomaly detection 4. Verify correct alerts fired This integration-level test across the three modules is missing.

recommendations.rec: Add historical scenario tests mirroring passes 4, 10, 11
Add RECOVERING and CANCELLING status parsing tests
Add alert deduplication test (check_all twice = zero new alerts)
Add budget alarm at both warning AND cap thresholds
Add manifest-to-detection integration test

expert.domain: DVC, DeepVess downloader, data integrity, GCS

analysis.finding: test_downloaders.py has 3 tests for VesselNN and likely 3-4 for DeepVess (added in this session), but missing: - Test that DeepVess ZIP extraction produces images/ and labels/ subdirs - Test that download_deepvess uses httpx (not requests or urllib) - Test that the eCommons URL constant matches the expected format - Test that skip_existing checks the images/ subdir (not .git)

The acquisition_registry.py was updated from download_method="manual" to "http_download" for DeepVess. No test verifies this value. A regression could silently revert it to "manual", breaking the acquisition flow.

test_dvc_remote_sync.py was modified with a new nfiles threshold but the test only checks dvc.yaml/dvc.lock consistency. No test verifies that BOTH MiniVess and DeepVess are tracked in DVC. The file counts (210+ for MiniVess, 26+ for DeepVess) should be asserted.

recommendations.rec: Extend DeepVess downloader tests for ZIP extraction structure
Test acquisition registry download_method values
Test DVC tracks both MiniVess and DeepVess datasets
Verify eCommons URL format is a valid DSpace 7 bitstream URL

expert.domain: Config drift, unauthorized GPUs, cost guardrails

analysis.finding: No test verifies the cost estimation logic. The preflight calculates per-pass cost as: n_jobs * time_per_job * hourly_rate. With the A100 fallback, incorrect hourly rates in yaml_contract.yaml could lead to budget overruns. Tests should verify: - L4 cost: 34 jobs * 55min * $0.22/hr = ~$6.85 - A100 cost: 34 jobs * 30min * $1.10/hr = ~$18.70 - A100-80GB cost: 34 jobs * 30min * $1.38/hr = ~$23.46 - Budget alarm triggers at correct thresholds

test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py has banned GPU tests but the test for "A100 not in GCP allowed list" was updated when A100 was authorized. We need a NEW test: "H100 is NOT in GCP allowed list" — the current highest-cost unauthorized GPU. Also: V100 ban test should exist.
No test verifies that gcp_spot.yaml's accelerator list matches the contract's GCP allowed list EXACTLY. Config drift (gcp_spot.yaml adding H100 while contract forbids it) would bypass the contract.
The use_spot per-model override in debug.yaml is a critical cost control (SAM3 on-demand prevents 80% preemption waste). No test verifies that SAM3 models have use_spot: false in model_overrides.

recommendations.rec: Test cost estimation for each GPU type against yaml_contract hourly rates
Test that H100, V100, T4 are NOT in GCP allowed list
Test gcp_spot.yaml accelerators match contract GCP list
Test SAM3 model_overrides have use_spot: false
Test budget warning and cap thresholds are set in factorial configs

council-review.consensus:
All 5 experts agree on 6 test categories with 42 tasks totaling ~80-100 new test functions. Priority order:
1. Region Migration Consistency (Cat 1) — P0: prevents $89/month egress recurrence 2. GPU Fallback and Contract (Cat 2) — P0: prevents 5.5x cost escalation 3. Monitoring Infrastructure (Cat 3) — P0: prevents 10h+ undetected failures 4. Data Pipeline (Cat 4) — P1: prevents broken acquisition flow 5. Infrastructure Resilience (Cat 5) — P1: prevents launch-time failures 6. Cost Guard Rails (Cat 6) — P1: prevents budget overruns
All tests target staging tier (no model loading, no cloud resources, no slow). Tests read configs from disk and use mock data — zero cloud dependency.
Test file organization: tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_region_consistency.py (Cat 1: ~15 tests) tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_gpu_fallback_contract.py (Cat 2: ~15 tests) tests/v2/unit/monitoring/test_anomaly_detector.py (Cat 3: ~15 tests, EXTEND existing) tests/v2/unit/monitoring/test_monitor_integration.py (Cat 3: ~5 tests, NEW) tests/v2/unit/test_downloaders.py (Cat 4: ~8 tests, EXTEND existing) tests/v2/unit/config/test_acquisition_registry.py (Cat 4: ~4 tests, NEW) tests/v2/unit/deployment/test_preflight_consistency.py (Cat 5: ~8 tests, NEW) tests/v2/unit/finops/test_cost_guardrails.py (Cat 6: ~10 tests, NEW)

category.name: Region Migration Consistency
category.rationale: Every region migration touches 15+ files. A single stale reference causes cross-region egress ($89/month) or FAILED_SETUP (wrong GAR URL). These tests form a regression safety net that catches stale references at make test-staging.

task.title: SkyPilot YAML image_id matches configured region
task.description: All SkyPilot YAML files with image_id: docker:... must reference the same GAR region as gcp_spot.yaml's docker_image field. Read both configs, extract the region prefix from the GAR URL, and assert they match.

tests.test: For each SkyPilot YAML in deployment/skypilot/*.yaml: Parse resources.image_id. Extract region from GAR URL pattern: {region}-docker.pkg.dev/... Load gcp_spot.yaml, extract region from docker_image. Assert regions match.
Collect all GAR URLs across all config files. Assert all extracted regions are identical.

task.green-command: N/A — tests read existing configs, no implementation needed. If tests fail, the configs are inconsistent and must be fixed.

task.title: GCS bucket references consistent across configs
task.description: The three GCS bucket names (dvc-data, mlflow-artifacts, checkpoints) appear in: scripts/preflight_gcp.py, dvc config files, .env.example, Pulumi outputs. All references must use the same bucket names.

tests.test: Read GCS_BUCKET from preflight_gcp.py source. Read DVC remote URL from .dvc/config or dvc.yaml. Assert they reference the same bucket.
Read CHECKPOINT_BUCKET from preflight_gcp.py. Verify this bucket name appears in relevant SkyPilot YAML setup scripts (if any reference GCS checkpoints).

task.title: No stale europe-west4 references in active configs
task.description: After migration to us-central1, zero active config files should reference europe-west4 as the primary region. Allowed exceptions: archived docs, metalearning files, comments documenting the migration history, and the europe_a100_fallback.yaml (which explicitly targets europe-west4 as EU fallback).

tests.test: For each deployment/skypilot/*.yaml: Read file content as text. Assert "europe-west4" does NOT appear in image_id or region fields. (Comments are allowed — filter to YAML-parsed values only.)
Parse configs/cloud/gcp_spot.yaml. Assert region != "europe-west4". Assert docker_image does not contain "europe-west4".
Read scripts/preflight_gcp.py as text. Assert the GAR_IMAGE constant does NOT contain "europe-west4".
Parse deployment/pulumi/gcp/Pulumi.dev.yaml. Assert gcp:region is not "europe-west4".

task.title: Region config regions match Pulumi-deployed region
task.description: The active region config (referenced in debug.yaml's infrastructure.region_config) must target the same region as the Pulumi stack.

tests.test: Read debug.yaml's infrastructure.region_config value (e.g., "us_central1"). Load configs/cloud/regions/{region_config}.yaml. Extract all region values from the YAML. Read Pulumi.dev.yaml's gcp:region. Assert ALL region config entries target the Pulumi region.
Read gcp_spot.yaml's region field. Read Pulumi.dev.yaml's gcp:region. Assert they match.

task.title: GAR URL in preflight_gcp.py matches gcp_spot.yaml
task.description: preflight_gcp.py has GAR_IMAGE as a hardcoded constant. It MUST match gcp_spot.yaml's docker_image. This test prevents drift after migrations.

tests.test: Import GAR_IMAGE from scripts/preflight_gcp.py (or parse from source using ast). Load gcp_spot.yaml's docker_image field. Assert they are identical.

task.title: Factorial YAML image_id matches gcp_spot docker_image
task.description: train_factorial.yaml's resources.image_id must reference the same Docker image as gcp_spot.yaml (the canonical cloud config).

tests.test: Parse train_factorial.yaml's resources.image_id (strip "docker:" prefix). Parse gcp_spot.yaml's docker_image. Assert they are identical.

category.name: GPU Fallback and Contract
category.rationale: The YAML contract system was created after the unauthorized A100 incident (5.5x cost). The contract was updated to allow A100 as an ordered fallback. Tests must verify: right GPUs, right order, right cost limits, right per-model spot overrides.

task.title: YAML contract allows exactly L4, A100, A100-80GB for GCP
task.description: The contract's allowed_accelerators.gcp list must contain exactly [L4, A100, A100-80GB] — no more, no less.

tests.test: Load yaml_contract.yaml. Assert allowed_accelerators.gcp == ["L4", "A100", "A100-80GB"].
Load yaml_contract.yaml. Assert "H100" not in allowed_accelerators.gcp.
Load yaml_contract.yaml. For each GPU in banned_accelerators (T4, V100, K80, P100, P4): Assert GPU not in allowed_accelerators.gcp.

task.title: Factorial contract allows exactly L4, A100, A100-80GB
task.description: The factorial section of the contract must also list L4, A100, A100-80GB. This is the cost-sensitive section (34+ jobs per pass).

tests.test: Load yaml_contract.yaml. Assert factorial.allowed_accelerators == ["L4", "A100", "A100-80GB"].
Assert factorial.required_cloud == "gcp".
Assert factorial.require_docker_image == True.

task.title: Region configs produce valid GPU ordering: L4 before A100
task.description: In ALL region config files, L4 entries MUST appear before A100 entries, which MUST appear before A100-80GB entries. SkyPilot's ordered: block tries entries in order — wrong order = A100 as primary (5x cost).

tests.test: For each configs/cloud/regions/*.yaml: Parse the regions.gcp dict. Collect GPU types in iteration order. If L4 and A100 both present: assert L4 index < A100 index. If L4 and A100-80GB both present: assert L4 index < A100-80GB index. If A100 and A100-80GB both present: assert A100 index < A100-80GB index.
Load us_central1.yaml. Assert first key in regions.gcp is "L4".
Load europe_a100_fallback.yaml. Assert first key in regions.gcp is "L4".

task.title: gcp_spot.yaml accelerators match contract GCP list
task.description: gcp_spot.yaml's accelerators list must be a subset of the contract's allowed_accelerators.gcp. No GPU in gcp_spot.yaml that isn't in the contract.

tests.test: Load gcp_spot.yaml, extract accelerator names (strip ":1" suffix). Load yaml_contract.yaml, get allowed_accelerators.gcp. Assert every gcp_spot accelerator is in the contract list.
Verify that the ordering in gcp_spot.yaml follows the same priority as the contract (L4 first, then A100, then A100-80GB).

task.title: use_spot override works per-model in factorial config
task.description: SAM3 models must have use_spot: false in model_overrides (80% preemption rate on 25+ min jobs makes spot economically irrational). DynUNet and MambaVesselNet should NOT have use_spot: false (they benefit from spot).

tests.test: Load debug.yaml. Assert model_overrides.sam3_hybrid.use_spot == False.
Load debug.yaml. Assert model_overrides.sam3_topolora.use_spot == False.
Load debug.yaml. Assert model_overrides does NOT contain a "dynunet" key with use_spot, OR if it does, use_spot is true/not set (allowing default spot behavior).
Load paper_full.yaml and debug.yaml. For each SAM3 model (sam3_hybrid, sam3_topolora): Assert use_spot value is identical in both configs. (Rule 27: debug = production for all factors.)

task.title: Max hourly cost per GPU type is set in contract
task.description: Every GPU type in the allowed list must have a max_hourly_cost_usd entry. This is the cost guardrail that preflight uses to estimate experiment cost.

tests.test: Load yaml_contract.yaml. For each GPU in allowed_accelerators.gcp + allowed_accelerators.runpod: Assert GPU appears as a key in max_hourly_cost_usd.
Assert max_hourly_cost_usd["L4"] < max_hourly_cost_usd["A100"].
Assert max_hourly_cost_usd["A100"] < max_hourly_cost_usd["A100-80GB"].

category.name: Monitoring Infrastructure
category.rationale: The monitoring infrastructure was implemented from scratch in this session. The existing 40 tests cover basics. These additional tests cover: - Historical failure scenario replay (passes 4, 10, 11) - Alert deduplication - Edge cases in duration parsing - Budget alarm at both thresholds - End-to-end manifest-to-detection flow

task.title: Replay pass 4 failure: 8 identical FAILED_SETUP triggers kill switch
task.description: On pass 4, 8 identical FAILED_SETUP jobs went undetected for 2h 20m. With the kill switch (threshold=3), the detector should fire KILL_SWITCH after the 3rd FAILED_SETUP.

tests.test: Create manifest with 8 jobs, all status=FAILED_SETUP, all with same failure_category="DOCKER_PULL_FAILED". Set kill_switch_threshold=3. Run check_all(). Assert at least one alert with alert_type="KILL_SWITCH_IDENTICAL" and severity="CRITICAL" and action="CANCEL_ALL".

task.title: Replay pass 10 failure: job running 12h for 5-min task triggers cancel
task.description: On pass 10, Job #154 ran 12+ hours for a 5-minute training task. cancel_duration_minutes=50 should trigger DURATION_CANCEL.

tests.test: Create manifest with 1 job: status=RUNNING, launched_at=12 hours ago, expected_duration_minutes=10, cancel_duration_minutes=50. Run check_all(). Assert alert with alert_type="DURATION_CANCEL", severity="CRITICAL", action="CANCEL_JOB".
Same manifest as above. Assert BOTH DURATION_WARN and DURATION_CANCEL alerts are present. DURATION_WARN fires at warn_duration (30 min), CANCEL at cancel (50 min).

task.title: Replay pass 11 failure: 3 jobs PENDING for 10h triggers timeout
task.description: On pass 11, 3 jobs were PENDING for 10+ hours in europe-west4 with zero intervention. pending_timeout_minutes=30 should trigger 3 PENDING_TIMEOUT alerts.

tests.test: Create manifest with 3 jobs: all status=PENDING, launched_at=10 hours ago, pending_timeout=30. Run check_all(). Assert exactly 3 alerts with alert_type="PENDING_TIMEOUT".

task.title: Alert deduplication: same alert not fired twice
task.description: The _already_fired set prevents duplicate alerts. Calling check_all() twice with the same manifest state should return zero alerts the second time.

tests.test: Create manifest with 1 PENDING job (launched 60 min ago, threshold 30). Create AnomalyDetector. Call check_all() — expect 1 PENDING_TIMEOUT alert. Call check_all() again — expect 0 new alerts (deduplicated).
Create manifest with 1 RUNNING job (launched 60 min ago, warn=30, cancel=50). Call check_all() — expect DURATION_WARN and DURATION_CANCEL. Both are different alert_types, so both should fire.

task.title: Budget alarm at warning AND cap thresholds
task.description: Budget warning fires BUDGET_WARN. Budget cap fires BUDGET_CAP with CANCEL_ALL action. Both must trigger when thresholds are crossed.

tests.test: Create manifest: budget_warning_usd=8.0, budget_cap_usd=15.0. Add jobs with accumulated cost = $9.00 (above warning, below cap). Assert BUDGET_WARN alert with severity=WARN.
Create manifest: budget_warning_usd=8.0, budget_cap_usd=15.0. Add jobs with accumulated cost = $16.00 (above cap). Assert BUDGET_CAP alert with severity=EMERGENCY, action=CANCEL_ALL.
Create manifest: budget_warning_usd=8.0. Add jobs with accumulated cost = $5.00 (below warning). Assert no budget-related alerts.

task.title: Kill switch triggers at 3+ identical failures
task.description: The kill switch fires when 3+ jobs have the same failure_category within the kill_switch_window_minutes window.

tests.test: Create manifest with 5 FAILED jobs, 3 having failure_category="OOM", 2 having "SPOT_PREEMPTION". Set kill_switch_threshold=3. Assert KILL_SWITCH_IDENTICAL alert fires (3 OOM).
Create manifest with 2 FAILED jobs with same failure_category. Set kill_switch_threshold=3. Assert NO kill switch alert fires.
Create manifest with 10 jobs, 6 FAILED (60%), 4 RUNNING. Set batch_failure_pct=0.5. Assert KILL_SWITCH_BATCH alert fires (60% > 50% threshold).

task.title: Sky queue parser handles RECOVERING and CANCELLING statuses
task.description: RECOVERING (spot preemption recovery) and CANCELLING (auto-cancel in progress) are valid SkyPilot statuses. The parser must handle them.

tests.test: Provide sky jobs queue output with a RECOVERING job. Assert parsed QueuedJob has status="RECOVERING".
Provide sky jobs queue output with a CANCELLING job. Assert parsed QueuedJob has status="CANCELLING".
Provide queue line with "1d 4h 55m 45s" total duration. Assert parsed total_duration_minutes is approximately 1735.75.

task.title: Monitor integration: manifest + queue update + anomaly detection
task.description: End-to-end test across all three monitoring modules: 1. Create a manifest with PENDING jobs 2. Simulate queue output showing jobs as RUNNING for too long 3. Update manifest from queue 4. Run anomaly detection 5. Verify correct alerts

tests.test: Create manifest JSON with 2 jobs (PENDING). Load manifest from JSON. Parse simulated sky jobs queue output (2 RUNNING, 1 exceeded warn). Update manifest job statuses from parsed queue. Create AnomalyDetector with updated manifest. Assert DURATION_WARN alert for the overrun job.
Create manifest with 3 jobs. Update all to SUCCEEDED. Assert ALL_TERMINAL alert with action=SUMMARY.

task.title: Job manifest round-trip with realistic 11th pass data
task.description: Serialize a manifest with the exact 11th pass job configuration (3 Phase 1 jobs: DynUNet spot, SAM3 Hybrid on-demand, SAM3 Vanilla spot) to JSON and deserialize. Verify all fields survive round-trip.

tests.test: Create manifest with 3 jobs matching 11th pass Phase 1: - dynunet-dice_ce-calibfalse-f0 (spot=True, expected=10, hourly_rate=0.22) - sam3_hybrid-cbdice_cldice-calibfalse-f0 (spot=False, expected=25, hourly_rate=0.74) - sam3_vanilla-zeroshot-minivess-f0 (spot=True, expected=15, hourly_rate=0.22) Write to JSON, read back. Assert all fields match.

category.name: Data Pipeline
category.rationale: DeepVess was treated as optional until this session. The user explicitly stated it is required for the Analysis flow. The downloader was implemented but needs comprehensive tests for ZIP extraction, skip-existing, and URL format. The acquisition registry change from "manual" to "http_download" must be tested to prevent silent regression.

task.title: DeepVess downloader: HTTP download from eCommons
task.description: download_deepvess() uses httpx to download a ZIP from Cornell eCommons. Test with mocked httpx to verify the URL, response handling, and ZIP extraction.

tests.test: Mock httpx.stream or httpx.Client. Call download_deepvess(tmp_path / "deepvess"). Assert httpx was called with the correct eCommons URL.
Import _DEEPVESS_ZIP_URL from downloaders. Assert URL contains "ecommons.cornell.edu/server/api/core/bitstreams/". Assert URL ends with "/content".

task.title: DeepVess downloader: skip if exists
task.description: When target_dir/images/ exists and has files, skip_existing=True should skip the download entirely.

tests.test: Create tmp_path/deepvess/images/ with a dummy .tif file. Call download_deepvess(tmp_path / "deepvess", skip_existing=True). Assert httpx was NOT called. Assert return value is the target_dir.
Create tmp_path/deepvess/images/ (empty directory). Call download_deepvess(..., skip_existing=True) with mocked httpx. Assert httpx WAS called (empty dir does not count as existing).

task.title: DeepVess in acquisition registry: download_method = "http_download"
task.description: The acquisition registry must have DeepVess registered with download_method="http_download" (not "manual"). This was changed in this session. A regression to "manual" would break the acquisition flow.

tests.test: Import ACQUISITION_REGISTRY or equivalent from acquisition_registry.py. Assert deepvess entry has download_method == "http_download".
Assert vesselnn entry has download_method == "git_clone".
Assert minivess entry has download_method == "manual" (EBRAINS requires login, cannot be automated).
For every entry in the acquisition registry: Assert "download_method" key exists. Assert value is one of the known methods ("http_download", "git_clone", "manual").

task.title: DVC tracks both MiniVess and DeepVess datasets
task.description: The DVC lock file must track both MiniVess (210+ files) and DeepVess (26+ files). Verify that both dataset paths appear in dvc.lock outputs.

tests.test: Parse dvc.lock. Assert at least one output path contains "minivess".
Parse dvc.lock. Assert at least one output path contains "deepvess".
If MiniVess is tracked as a directory output in dvc.lock: Read the nfiles field. Assert nfiles >= 200 (210 files expected).
If DeepVess is tracked as a directory output in dvc.lock: Read the nfiles field. Assert nfiles >= 20 (26 files expected).

category.name: Infrastructure Resilience
category.rationale: The preflight script is the gate between "ready to launch" and "spending money on GPU instances." Every observed FAILED_SETUP root cause should have a corresponding preflight check test. These tests use mocked subprocess calls — no cloud resources needed.

task.title: Preflight detects stale Docker image (git commit mismatch)
task.description: When Docker image OCI label revision differs from git HEAD and src/ files changed, preflight should return FAIL.

tests.test: Mock subprocess to return a Docker manifest with revision="abc123". Mock git HEAD as "def456". Mock git diff showing src/ changes between abc123 and def456. Call check_docker_image_freshness() (or equivalent). Assert result is (False, message_containing "stale").
Mock subprocess to return revision="abc123", HEAD="def456". Mock git diff showing ONLY docs/ and tests/ changes. Assert result is (True, message_containing "no Docker-relevant").

task.title: Preflight config references match across files
task.description: The preflight script's hardcoded constants (GAR_IMAGE, GCS_BUCKET, CHECKPOINT_BUCKET, SKYPILOT_YAML) must match the canonical config sources. This prevents the preflight from checking the wrong resources.

tests.test: Read GAR_IMAGE from preflight_gcp.py source (using ast.parse). Read docker_image from gcp_spot.yaml. Assert they are identical.
Read SKYPILOT_YAML path from preflight_gcp.py source. Assert the file exists on disk.
Read REQUIRED_ENV_VARS from preflight_gcp.py source. Assert "HF_TOKEN" is in the list. Assert "MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI" is in the list.

task.title: YAML contract validator catches unauthorized GPU types
task.description: Test the validation logic that catches unauthorized GPUs in SkyPilot YAMLs. This is already partially tested in test_yaml_contract_enforcement.py but we need explicit tests for the NEW fallback GPUs.

tests.test: Create a temporary SkyPilot YAML with accelerators: H100:1. Run the contract validation function. Assert validation FAILS with message about unauthorized GPU.
Create a temporary SkyPilot YAML with ordered: block containing L4 and A100 entries (both in contract). Run contract validation. Assert validation PASSES.
Create a temporary SkyPilot YAML with T4 in an ordered: block. Assert validation FAILS (T4 is globally banned).

task.title: Region config files parse correctly and have required fields
task.description: Every YAML file in configs/cloud/regions/ must parse without error and contain the required structure: description, regions.gcp.{GPU}.[{region, zones}].

tests.test: For each configs/cloud/regions/*.yaml: yaml.safe_load succeeds. No exceptions raised.
For each region config: Assert "description" key exists.
For each region config: Assert "regions" key exists. Assert "gcp" key exists under "regions".
For each region config, for each GPU type, for each entry: Assert entry has "region" (string) and "zones" (int > 0).

category.name: Cost Guard Rails
category.rationale: The FinOps report (gpu-instances-finops-report.md) established cost models for each GPU type. The YAML contract has max_hourly_cost_usd entries. These tests verify the cost estimation logic and guard rails.

task.title: Cost estimate calculation correct for L4, A100, A100-80GB
task.description: Verify the per-job and per-pass cost calculations match the FinOps report. Formula: cost_per_job = (setup_minutes + training_minutes) / 60 * hourly_rate Formula: cost_per_pass = cost_per_job * n_jobs

tests.test: hourly_rate = 0.22 (L4 spot). total_minutes = 55 (10 setup + 45 training). Expected cost = 55/60 * 0.22 = ~$0.20. Assert abs(cost - 0.2017) < 0.01.
hourly_rate = 1.10 (A100-40GB spot). total_minutes = 30 (10 setup + 20 training at 2.2x speedup). Expected cost = 30/60 * 1.10 = $0.55. Assert abs(cost - 0.55) < 0.01.
hourly_rate = 1.38 (A100-80GB spot). total_minutes = 30. Expected cost = 30/60 * 1.38 = $0.69. Assert abs(cost - 0.69) < 0.01.
34 jobs * $0.20/job = ~$6.85. Assert abs(total - 6.86) < 0.5.
34 jobs * $0.55/job = ~$18.70. Assert abs(total - 18.70) < 1.0.

task.title: JobRecord cost_usd property calculates correctly
task.description: JobRecord.cost_usd must compute elapsed_hours * hourly_rate. Test with known durations and rates.

tests.test: Create JobRecord with launched_at 15 min ago, hourly_rate=0.22. Assert cost_usd is approximately 0.22 * 15/60 = $0.055.
Create JobRecord with ended_at 2 hours after launched_at, hourly_rate=1.10. Assert cost_usd is approximately $2.20.
Create JobRecord with launched_at=None. Assert cost_usd == 0.0.

task.title: Manifest total_cost sums all job costs correctly
task.description: JobManifest.total_cost must sum cost_usd across all jobs.

tests.test: Create manifest with 3 jobs: - Job 1: 30 min at $0.22/hr = $0.11 - Job 2: 60 min at $1.10/hr = $1.10 - Job 3: 15 min at $0.22/hr = $0.055 Assert total_cost is approximately $1.265.
Create manifest with 3 PENDING jobs (launched_at = just now). Assert total_cost is approximately $0.00.

task.title: Contract max_hourly_cost values are sane
task.description: The max_hourly_cost_usd values in yaml_contract.yaml must be within reasonable bounds and maintain the correct ordering (L4 cheapest).

tests.test: Load yaml_contract.yaml. Assert max_hourly_cost_usd["L4"] < 1.0.
Assert max_hourly_cost_usd["A100"] < 2.0.
Assert L4 < RTX4090 < A100 < A100-80GB < H100.
For every GPU in max_hourly_cost_usd: Assert value > 0.

task.title: Factorial configs have budget warning and cap thresholds
task.description: Both debug.yaml and paper_full.yaml should reference budget thresholds either directly or through the monitoring manifest config. At minimum, verify the existence of budget alarm fields in the report template.

tests.test: Load debug.yaml. Assert the estimated cost section or the report template references budget_warning and budget_cap values. (This may read from the 11th pass report template fields.)
Compute: 34 jobs * max A100-80GB rate ($1.38/hr) * 1 hour max. Assert total < $50 (academic budget floor).

step.reason: P0: Region drift is the #1 recurrence risk
P0: GPU contract prevents 5.5x cost escalation

final-verification.expected: ALL tests pass (existing + new), 0 failures, 0 skipped
final-verification.notes: New test count should increase the staging total by ~80-100 tests. If any existing test breaks due to config changes, fix it as part of this plan. Report final test count: before and after.

cross-references.ref: Prevention: Test that DeepVess is NOT optional — it must be in DVC and acquisition registry. Covered by: T4.3, T4.4
Prevention: Test that DeepVess has automated download_method, not "manual". Covered by: T4.3
Prevention: Test that anomaly detector fires PENDING_TIMEOUT after threshold. Covered by: T3.3, T3.4
Prevention: Test that contract allows ONLY authorized GPUs, in correct order. Covered by: T2.1, T2.2, T2.3, T2.6
Prevention: Contract golden reference is the single source of truth. Covered by: T2.4, T5.3
Cost models and GPU pricing verified by: T6.1, T6.4
Region migration consistency tested by: T1.1-T1.6
